title: pseudoheterocaryum macrocarpum
intro: Pseudoheterocaryum macrocarpum, also known as the desert caltrop or devil's thorn, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Zygophyllaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pseudoheterocaryum macrocarpum include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as having antioxidant and diuretic effects. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the leaves or roots and using them in infusions or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
